# Preface {.unnumbered}

This companion book contains my code and interpretation for the 279 computer exercises found in the main textbook along with tips and notes. Hope that it serves as an educational reference.

## About the Author

I am Ahmed H. Darwish, a Teaching Assistant with a Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, majoring in Applied Statistics. My work focuses on econometrics and causal inference.

## Exercises Solved

```{r}
#| label: progress
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Click to see how the tracker is generated"

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(gtExtras)


progress_data <- tribble(
  ~Chapter, ~Questions, ~Solved,
  1, 9, 9,
  2, 12, 8,
  3, 14, 3,
  4, 15, 3,
  5, 7, 3,
  6, 16, 2,
  7, 19, 0,
  8, 14, 0,
  9, 15, 0,
  10, 15, 0,
  11, 15, 0,
  12, 17, 0,
  13, 18, 0,
  14, 20, 0,
  15, 15, 0,
  16, 12, 0,
  17, 18, 0,
  18, 18, 0,
  19, 10, 0
) |> 
  mutate(
    Percentage = Solved / Questions,
    Status = case_when(
      Percentage == 1 ~ "Finished",
      Percentage > 0  ~ "In Progress",
      TRUE            ~ "Pending"
    )
  )


progress_data |>
  gt() |>
  
  gt_plt_bar_pct(column = Percentage, scaled = FALSE, fill = "#2c3e50", background = "#ecf0f1") |>
  
  gt_badge(Status, palette = c("Finished" = "#27ae60", "In Progress" = "#f39c12", "Pending" = "#bdc3c7")) |>
  cols_label(
    Percentage = "Progress",
    Solved = "Completed"
  ) |>
  tab_header(
    title = md("**Exercise Completion Tracker**"),
    subtitle = "Real-time status of the Wooldridge Solution Manual"
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.width = pct(90),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.title.font.size = px(24)
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())
```

::: callout-note
8th edition increased computer exercises from 246 to 279
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*This project is a work in progress as part of my portfolio. For collaborations or inquiries regarding remote data analysis, feel free to contact me via GitHub.*
